title: Build REST API server from data model
goal:
Build a simple REST API using Python's built-in http.server (no frameworks).

The API should manage a "books" collection with these endpoints:
- GET /books — list all books
- GET /books/<id> — get one book
- POST /books — create a book (JSON body: title, author, year)
- DELETE /books/<id> — delete a book

Requirements:
1. Create server.py implementing the API
2. Store data in books.json (persist between requests)
3. Return proper status codes (200, 201, 404)
4. Return JSON responses with Content-Type header
5. Create test_api.py that starts the server, runs all CRUD tests,
   and shuts down the server
6. Run the tests to verify everything works
ground_truth: server.py with all 4 endpoints working, test_api.py that validates CRUD operations

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Clear any pre-existing data
rm -f books.json

# Start server
python3 server.py &
SERVER_PID=$!
sleep 2

cleanup() { kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null; wait $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true; }
trap cleanup EXIT

# Test POST - create a book
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST http://localhost:8000/books -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"title":"Test Book","author":"Author","year":2024}')
if [ "$CODE" != "201" ]; then echo "POST failed: expected 201, got $CODE"; exit 1; fi

# Test GET all - should have at least 1 book (handle list or {books: list} format)
LIST=$(curl -s http://localhost:8000/books)
COUNT=$(echo "$LIST" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
if isinstance(d, list): books = d
elif isinstance(d, dict): books = d.get('books', d.get('data', list(d.values())[0] if d else []))
else: books = []
if not isinstance(books, list): books = []
print(len(books))
" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
if [ "$COUNT" -lt 1 ]; then echo "GET /books returned no books"; exit 1; fi

# Test 404 for non-existent book
NF_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8000/books/99999)
if [ "$NF_CODE" != "404" ]; then echo "Expected 404 for /books/99999, got $NF_CODE"; exit 1; fi

echo "All API tests passed"
